topic: Google Tasks to Obsidian Vault Sync

sources.title: Google Tasks API: tasks.list reference
sources.key_findings: Supports updatedMin (RFC 3339) for incremental fetch, showDeleted, showHidden, showCompleted flags. No syncToken mechanism. Rate limit: 50,000 queries/day.

sources.title: Google Tasks API: tasks.patch reference
sources.key_findings: PATCH with {status: completed} is idempotent, re-patching a completed task is a no-op. Auto-populates completed timestamp. Requires tasks scope.

sources.title: Google Tasks API: tasks.delete reference
sources.key_findings: DELETE cascades to assigned tasks in Docs/Chat Spaces. Recurring tasks re-create with new ID after deletion. Avoid for general sync cleanup.

sources.title: Google Tasks API: Task resource schema
sources.key_findings: Fields: id, title, notes (max 8192 chars), status, due (RFC3339, time always T00:00:00.000Z), completed, updated, deleted, hidden, parent, etag, position, links, webViewLink.

sources.title: Google Workspace Events API
sources.key_findings: Covers Chat, Meet, Drive — Tasks is NOT a subscribable resource type. No push notification support for Google Tasks as of 2026.

sources.title: Vault: Task Management System.md
sources.key_findings: Authoritative 16-section reference. Task format: - [STATE] PRIORITY Description ⏳/📅 YYYY-MM-DD #tag. Four system tags: #waiting, #someday, #theirs, #agenda. ⏳=scheduled(intent), 📅=due(hard deadline).

sources.title: Vault: CLAUDE.md
sources.key_findings: Vault overview with task syntax snippet and structure summary.

sources.title: Vault: Tasks Dashboard queries
sources.key_findings: 7 dashboard views. Today: scheduled on today OR due on today. Overdue: due before today. System tags excluded from specific views. Synced tasks must satisfy these queries.

sources.title: g-api-mcp: tasks.py
sources.key_findings: tasks_list_tasks already surfaces updated timestamp. _to_thin_task()/_to_full_task() pattern is the model for _to_vault_line(). No updatedMin parameter exposed yet.

sources.title: g-api-mcp: auth.py
sources.key_findings: GoogleCredentialManager singleton, Windows Credential Locker (keyring/DPAPI), auto-refresh. Works in CLI context (no MCP server needed). Reusable by sync.py.

sources.title: g-api-mcp: tests/test_tasks.py
sources.key_findings: pytest + pytest-asyncio. Mock pattern: patch.object(tasks_module, '_tasks_service'). Pure-function unit tests for _to_thin_task/_to_full_task. Same pattern for _to_vault_line.

sources.title: Google Tasks API: tasks.clear reference
sources.key_findings: tasks.clear sets hidden=true on all completed tasks. No automatic purge timeline documented. Hidden tasks persist and are retrievable with showHidden=true.
